mission.title: helm: UPGRADE FAILED: No resource with the name "" found
mission.description: UPGRADE FAILED: No resource with the name "" found. This issue affects 124+ users.

steps.title: Identify helm troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your helm deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=helm
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=helm -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `UPGRADE FAILED: Looks like there are no changes for cm1`

steps.title: Check current Configmap configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant helm resources:
```bash
kubectl get configmap -A
kubectl describe configmap <name> -n <namespace>
```
## Repro

Create a simple Chart.yaml:

``` yaml
name: upgrade-repro
version: 0.1.0
```

With a single K8S resource in the `templates/` dir:

``` yaml
kind: ConfigMap
apiVersion: v1
metadata:
  name: c

steps.title: Apply the fix for UPGRADE FAILED: No resource with the name "" found
steps.description:
This fixes #1193 by not making an internal cache miss an error, and instead making it a warning. It will default to the original object, if one exists, instead. This should fix at least one scenario of when helm loses track of the real state of the a config.

```yaml
With a single K8S resource in the `templates/` dir:
```

steps.title: Confirm UPGRADE FAILED: No resource with the name "" found is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=helm -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `UPGRADE FAILED: Looks like there are no changes for cm1` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: This fixes #1193 by not making an internal cache miss an error, and instead making it a warning. It will default to the original object, if one exists, instead. This should fix at least one scenario of when helm loses track of the real state of the a config.

This could have issues since it uses the existing object in place of the cached one. Let me know if there are better ways of handling this, . This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with helm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
